title: gratiola saginoides
intro: Gratiola saginoides, also known as Dragonwort or Sneezeweed, is a perennial plant native to North America that belongs to the family Scrophulariaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, due to its medicinal properties such as antispasmodic, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Gratiola saginoides include relief from coughs, colds, bronchitis, and asthma, as well as its use in treating skin conditions and as a natural remedy for snakebites. Herbal preparations of Gratiola saginoides typically involve drying the leaves and stems, which can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
